_note: TKT-0083 / ADR-0146 — the live-turn lifecycle channel. ONE manifest, TWO sequenced slice groups (ADR-0146 Sequencing): Slice A (n2-n8, component/UI — ships against today's wire, closes the blank-bubble symptom immediately) then Slice B (n9-n13, pipeline — the progress wire). One manifest, not two, because the TurnProgress vocabulary is one shared spine both slices consume — two manifests would duplicate it and invite drift. OUTSIDE-IN nodes = the surfaces touched (timeline-item status vocab, status-stream header/grouping, conversation live narration, meta-line envelope, provider seam, produce loop, recorded transport, proxy+consumer loops). INSIDE-OUT actions = what the system must newly DO, crossed via hosts. plan:true — every leaf carries a checkable accept predicate. The grouping leg (n4) is explicitly sequenced on ADR-0143's build landing first (its [data-role=nested] slot is the mechanism reused, never duplicated). validate-then-stream (SPEC-R5) is UNTOUCHED throughout — progress is not content. A-before-B is a DELIVERY-PRIORITY ruling (the user-visible fix ships first), not a technical dependency — the graph deliberately leaves nB's leaves parallel-startable except where a real edge exists (n0→n4 gating grouping on ADR-0143's build; n8→n13, the cross-slice seam).

nodes.label: live-turn lifecycle feedback — real signals reach a live, grouped, header-carrying narration surface
SLICE A — component/UI: richer vocabulary + header + grouping + live-at-ingest narration (no wire dependency)
EXTERNAL GATE — ADR-0143's nested-slot build has shipped (the mechanism n4 reuses)
nodes.accept: `grep -q 'data-role="nested"' packages/agent-ui/components/src/controls/timeline-item/timeline-item.ts` exits 0 AND ADR-0143's own build gates are green in the tree — this leaf is satisfied by ANOTHER ticket's build (TKT-0091), never by this one; it exists so the graph (not just prose) blocks n4 until the reused mechanism is real

nodes.label: ui-timeline-item: the `warning` status member (ADR-0146 F7) — enum + distinct non-color glyph + CSS + descriptor
nodes.accept: `npm test timeline-item` — the STATUS enum is exactly ['', 'pending', 'active', 'done', 'error', 'warning']; `warning` renders its own marker glyph distinct from error's `x` (ADR-0057: shape-coded, never hue-alone), the descriptor trip-wire (timeline-item-descriptor.test.ts) and family-coherence stay green; `npm run test:browser timeline-item` proves the glyph legible under forced-colors: active

nodes.label: ui-status-stream: the opt-in visible header (ADR-0146 F8) — reflected `header` prop, [data-part=header] row, label + live overall-status glyph
nodes.accept: `npm test status-stream` — header:false (default) renders byte-identically to today (regression guard: zero visible header DOM); header:true shows the label text VISIBLY plus an overall-status glyph following ADR-0146 F8's one rule — while un-finalized: the escalated top-level status when it OUTRANKS `active` (a mid-turn `error` child flips the header immediately, F6's monotone-truth), else `active` (so an EMPTY un-finalized stream reads 'working' from construction — the blank-bubble root fix); finalize() settles it to the escalated final status, fail() to `error`; the header sits OUTSIDE the scroll region (never scrolls away) — a browser probe asserts it stays pinned while entries overflow

nodes.label: ui-status-stream: grouped entries (ADR-0146 F5) — StatusEntry.parent mounts a nested ui-timeline into the parent item's [data-role=nested] slot (ADR-0143's mechanism, reused)
nodes.accept: `npm test status-stream` — appendEntry({key, parent}) creates the child item INSIDE a nested <ui-timeline> mounted in the parent item's [data-role=nested] slot (lazily, once per parent); the keyed registry stays FLAT (update(childKey, patch) reaches a nested entry identically; duplicate keys rejected/ignored exactly as today); the shared ui-disclosure collapse + ADR-0143 F3 collapsed-summary preview work on the group with ZERO new nesting code in this build; finalize() truncation reaches nested pending/active entries. SEQUENCED: graph-blocked on n0 (ADR-0143's nested-slot build shipped — the n0→n4 edge)

nodes.label: status escalation (ADR-0146 F6) — worst-child-wins over error > warning > active > pending > done, recomputed live, painted on the group header row and the stream header
nodes.accept: `npm test status-stream` — a group with children [done, warning] reads `warning`; [active, error] reads `error` (the ladder's error-beats-active case asserted explicitly); neutral '' children contribute nothing; escalation recomputes live on a child's status change (corrected 2026-07-18 per ADR-0146's own F6 amendment: recomputed IMPERATIVELY from ui-status-stream's own appendEntry/update calls, NOT via a MutationObserver — the ADR-0143 F3 observer stays reserved for the collapsed-summary preview alone); the stream-level header (n3) escalates over TOP-LEVEL entries only

nodes.label: aria-live discipline under grouping — role=log announces genuine additions once, nested appends never double-announce
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser status-stream` — appending a parented entry produces ONE addition announcement path (the nested ui-timeline is role=list inside the outer role=log; the probe asserts no duplicated live-region semantics on the nested host), status PATCHES still do not re-announce (the shipped role=log addition-relevance discipline holds with nesting present)

nodes.label: ui-conversation: narration goes live-at-ingest (SPEC-R6 amendment) — categories narrate as ingestLine() sees them; the finalize() replay + NARRATION_STEP_MS pacing are removed, not stranded; the strip opts into the header
nodes.accept: `npm test conversation` — a category entry appears when its FIRST line of that category is ingested (pending/active during the turn), settles at finalize(); the amended SPEC-R6 AC1 asserts entries 'as the turn's lines are ingested' in emission order; NARRATION_STEP_MS and the post-hoc narrateCategories replay are DELETED (grep-zero); the narration strip sets header on its composed ui-status-stream, so a turn with ZERO lines and ZERO progress still shows a visible working header at t=0 — the blank-bubble regression proof; note/summarize()/disclosure behavior byte-unchanged

nodes.label: AgentTurnHandle.progress(ev) — the fifth handle method (SPEC §4 widening, recorded by amendment) routing TurnProgress into the strip via a closed code-owned stage-label table
nodes.accept: `npm test conversation` — handle.progress({stage:'reasoning'}) renders the fixed 'Reasoning…' entry (stage → label from ONE closed table; no model text, no invented prose — the amended honesty-law guard asserted as a negative control: an unknown stage renders NOTHING); 'retry' carries the round ordinal factually; progress entries live in the same strip as category entries and settle under the same finalize()/fail() completion invariant; a consumer that never calls progress() is byte-behavior-unchanged

nodes.label: SLICE B — pipeline: real lifecycle signals from the provider through produce() over the proxy into the consumer loops
meta-line.ts: the `progress` envelope kind — {"a2uiMeta":{"progress":{stage,round?,detail?}}}, readMetaLine round-trip, fault isolation intact
nodes.accept: `npm test meta-line` — readMetaLine round-trips the progress kind (closed stage union; malformed progress ⇒ envelope returns WITHOUT it, note/trace/ask unaffected); a progress line reaching the renderer's dispatch() unfiltered still routes to VERSION_UNSUPPORTED, returned not thrown (the ADR-0088 defense-in-depth regression, extended to the new kind)

nodes.label: AgentProvider.onEvent (additive, the effort? precedent) + the Anthropic adapter maps message_start/content_block_start/thinking-delta/message_stop onto it
nodes.accept: `npm test anthropic-sse` — the pure parse layer, fed the captured SSE fixture WITH lifecycle frames, invokes onEvent with the mapped ProviderEvent sequence while yielding the identical text fragments as today (byte-identical accumulation — a regression guard); a caller passing NO onEvent gets today's behavior exactly; thinking deltas map to {kind:'thinking', text} (forwarding gated later at n11, not here); no @anthropic-ai/sdk import appears (SPEC-R3 grep stays green)

nodes.label: produce(): interleaved progress yields (sent → started → reasoning → content → validating → retry(n) → done) + the progressDetail gate; validate-then-stream preserved exactly
nodes.accept: `npm test produce-loop` — a stub provider driving onEvent makes produce() yield progress meta-lines AS THEY HAPPEN, strictly BEFORE any content line, and every A2UI content line still streams only after the whole payload validates (SPEC-R5 asserted in the same test); {stage:'content'} is emitted by produce() on the round's OWN first text fragment (the one pinned emitter of that stage — ADR-0146 F1); a self-correct round yields {stage:'retry', round}; progressDetail absent ⇒ NO detail text on reasoning events (the F3 default asserted as a negative control), 'full' forwards bounded excerpts; note-only turns and ProduceHalt behavior byte-unchanged

nodes.label: recorded transport: RecordedTurn.progress?: TurnProgress[] replayed ahead of lines — the keyless demo demonstrates staged feedback
nodes.accept: `npm test recorded-transport && npm test round-trip` — a turn authored with progress events replays them as the same meta-line shape the live path emits, ahead of the turn's lines (SPEC-R5/N4 parity); a progress-less turn streams byte-identically to today; the committed demo transcript gains plausible authored stages so the docs site's default keyless demo shows the feature (structural-transcript gates stay green)

nodes.label: the proxy carries progress lines unchanged; consumer loops (a2ui-chat.ts, a2ui-live.ts, admin-live-runner.ts) route meta.progress → handle.progress()
nodes.accept: `npm run check && npm test` — dev-proxy-plugin.ts needs no structural change (progress lines flow through the existing per-line res.write; verified by a produce-stub proxy test asserting NDJSON interleaving, flush per line); each consumer loop's existing readMetaLine filter grows the progress arm (routes to handle.progress, never to ingestLine/host.ingest/the JSON tab/corpus paths); agent-admin's admin-live-runner routes it too — TKT-0083's own flagged at-build check, closed here, not assumed

nodes.label: gates + records: SPEC/LLD repairs at build, per-slice independent review, standing gates green
nodes.accept: per slice: `npm run check && npm test` (+ `npm run test:browser` for the component slice) green with no key; the three spec amendments' 'no build has landed' sentences updated as each slice lands; descriptor/family-coherence/prompt-drift/round-trip gates green; a fresh-context independent reviewer returns GO per slice BEFORE its commit (generator ≠ critic); TKT-0083 Findings updated per slice

actions.label: express a lesser advisory outcome distinct from error, icon-coded, never color-only
show the turn's live overall status visibly from t=0, pinned outside the scroll region, settling with the completion invariant
nest a parented entry under its group via ADR-0143's slot + shared disclosure, keys staying flat
escalate a group's (and the stream's) status worst-child-wins over one closed ladder, live
announce grouped additions politely, exactly once, with no nested double-announcement
narrate categories at ingest time, deleting the post-hoc replay and its pacing constant
route TurnProgress events into the strip through a closed, code-owned stage-label table
carry the progress kind on the reserved envelope with the versionless fault isolation intact
surface the provider's real lifecycle events through an additive optional callback, text accumulation unchanged
yield progress lines during the round loop while content still streams only after full validation
keep raw reasoning text off the wire by default, forwarded only behind an explicit opt-in
replay authored synthetic stages keylessly so the default demo demonstrates the feature
carry progress over the dev proxy and route it in every consumer loop, including agent-admin's
gate each slice on green standing gates plus an independent reviewer GO before commit
block the grouping leg on ADR-0143's shipped nested-slot mechanism, as a graph edge, not prose

edges.why: grouping REUSES ADR-0143's [data-role=nested] slot — n4 is graph-blocked until that external build ships (review finding 1: the gate is an edge, not prose)
the escalation ladder (n5) includes the warning member n2 adds
the stream-level escalation paints into the header row n3 builds
group escalation needs grouped children to escalate over
the double-announcement risk only exists once nesting (n4) exists
ui-conversation's strip opts into the header n3 builds
progress entries land in the live-at-ingest strip n7 rewires; the handle widening composes on it
produce() emits the envelope kind n9 defines
produce() composes the provider events n10 surfaces into its own stage stream
the recorded replay emits the same envelope shape n9 defines (SPEC-R5/N4 parity)
the proxy/consumer routing carries what produce() now yields
consumer loops route meta.progress to the handle.progress method n8 adds — the cross-slice seam, fixed by ADR-0146 so the slices cannot drift
the component slice's review gates on the vocabulary landing
the component slice's review gates on the live-at-ingest rewiring landing
the pipeline slice's review gates on the end-to-end routing landing
